title: glycine soja f. ovata
intro: Glycine soja f. ovata, also known as the Japanese soybean or wild soybean, is a plant species belonging to the Leguminosae family and is closely related to soybeans. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Glycine soja f. ovata include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an anti-aging agent. Herbal preparations of Glycine soja f. ovata typically involve infusions, decoctions, or extracts of its leaves, stems, and seeds, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, from digestive issues to skin problems.
